title: Shorea gardneri
intro: Shorea gardneri, commonly known as the Burmese rosewood or the Burmese shorea, is a tropical tree species native to Southeast Asia, belonging to the family Dipterocarpaceae. The plant has been traditionally used in medicine for its numerous health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which have been shown to aid in wound healing, reduce fever, and alleviate respiratory issues. The bioactive constituents of Shorea gardneri include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and terpenoids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional medicine, Shorea gardneri is often used to prepare herbal remedies such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, as well as being incorporated into various Ayurvedic and Unani formulations.
taxonomy: Shorea gardneri is classified as a species of tree within the family Dipterocarpaceae. This family is commonly known as the dipterocarp family. The genus Shorea is a group of large trees found in tropical forests of Asia, particularly in countries such as India, Malaysia, and Indonesia. The plant's extracts are used to treat various health issues, including fever, cough, and respiratory problems. In addition, Shorea gardneri is also used to treat wounds and skin infections due to its antiseptic properties.
